reasoning:
Short-term (Jun 29): The index is currently at 0.74 and has flatlined exactly at 0.74 for the four trading days leading up to the forecast date. Because the resolution requires the spread to strictly increase (i.e. >0.74), and a flat result resolves as NO, the near-term probability is materially below 50% due to inertia (P(flat or decrease) > P(increase)). 

Medium-term (Jul 22 & Sep 20): As the horizon expands to 1-3 months, random walk variance increases, sharply reducing the probability of the value lingering exactly at 0.74. The trend from April's spike (0.99) has been strongly downward, maintaining pressure below 0.50. However, because 0.74 is relatively tight historically, upward mean reversion creates a countering force. I have scaled the probabilities toward maximum uncertainty (0.50). 

Long-term (Dec 19): Over a 6-month period, short-term trends wash out and uncertainty is highest. A spread of 0.74 is low historically, so mean reversion pushes the long-term probability of an increase to at least 50%. The probability is conservatively pegged at 0.50 to reflect maximal long-horizon uncertainty.
